pg37-p1: easily pushed aside in favor of the foolish idea that he could take whiskey if only he mixed it with milk!
pg37-p2-s1: Whatever the precise definition of the word may be, we call this plain insanity.
pg37-p2-s2:
How can such a lack of proportion, of
the ability to think straight, be called anything else?
pg37-p2:
Whatever the precise definition of the word may be, we call this plain insanity. How can such a lack of proportion, of
the ability to think straight, be called anything else?
pg37-p3-s1: You may think this an extreme case.
pg37-p3-s2:
To us it is not far-fetched, for this kind of thinking has been characteristic of
every single one of us.
pg37-p3-s3: We have sometimes reflected more than Jim did upon the consequences.
pg37-p3-s4:
But there was always the
curious mental phenomenon that parallel with our sound reasoning there inevitably ran some insanely trivial excuse for
taking the first drink.
pg37-p3-s5: Our sound reasoning failed to hold us in check.
pg37-p3-s7:
Next day we would ask
ourselves, in all earnestness and sincerity, how it could have happened.
pg37-p3:
You may think this an extreme case. To us it is not far-fetched, for this kind of thinking has been characteristic of
every single one of us. We have sometimes reflected more than Jim did upon the consequences. But there was always the
curious mental phenomenon that parallel with our sound reasoning there inevitably ran some insanely trivial excuse for
taking the first drink. Our sound reasoning failed to hold us in check. The insane idea won out. Next day we would ask
ourselves, in all earnestness and sincerity, how it could have happened.
pg37-p4-s1:
In some circumstances we have gone out deliberately to get drunk, feeling ourselves justified by nervousness, anger,
worry, depression, jealousy or the like.
pg37-p4-s2:
But even in this type of beginning we are obliged to admit that our
justification for a spree was insanely insufficient in the light of what always happened.
pg37-p4-s3:
We now see that when we began
to drink deliberately, instead of casually, there was little serious or effective thought during the period of
premeditation of what the terrific consequences might be.
pg37-p4:
In some circumstances we have gone out deliberately to get drunk, feeling ourselves justified by nervousness, anger,
worry, depression, jealousy or the like. But even in this type of beginning we are obliged to admit that our
justification for a spree was insanely insufficient in the light of what always happened. We now see that when we began
to drink deliberately, instead of casually, there was little serious or effective thought during the period of
premeditation of what the terrific consequences might be.
pg37-p5-s1:
Our behavior is as absurd and incomprehensible with respect to the first drink as that of an individual with a passion,
say, for jay-walking.
pg37-p5-s2: He gets a thrill out of skipping in front of fast-moving vehicles.
pg37-p5-s3:
He enjoys himself for a few
years in spite of friendly warnings.
pg37-p5-s4: Up to this point you would label him as a foolish
